headline: The Tribe Keeps Two Fires
deck: American forces struck more than eighty Iranian targets after tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, Brent repriced the chokepoint, and FedWatch showed a firmer rate path in the same session. A second fire burned at the World Cup, where heat thresholds, hydration breaks, and schedule pressure turned summer weather into ritual stress. The shared object is attention architecture, with no hidden hand required.
body: On 7 July, tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz drew American retaliation against more than eighty Iranian targets in a single retaliatory round [E1]. The next session, Brent repriced the chokepoint as the interim accord was pronounced over and crude climbed toward $78 [E2][E3]. FedWatch put September move odds at 65.7%, up from 61.9% before the minutes; the record can say policy expectations firmed on the day, not that oil alone caused it [E4]. Literal fire, barrel price, and policy odds stacked on one front page without waiting for a single theory to bind them [E1][E2][E4].

The second fire was heat. FIFPRO's guidance marks 26°C WBGT as cooling-break territory and 28°C as the point where postponement should be considered [E5]. World Weather Attribution projected 26 of 104 matches at or above the lower threshold, and FIFA answered tournament-wide with mandatory hydration breaks rather than a lower postponement line [E6][E7]. The public record supports heat thresholds, hydration policy, and schedule-management choices; it does not prove the whole broadcast motive, only that World Cup timing carried visible weather strain [E5][E6][E8]. If the screen is still the hearth the tribe never left, then the World Cup feed and the strait headline share architecture, not conspiracy [E9].

Polly Wiessner places firelight talk at the hours when practical day-speech gave way to story, ritual, and imagined others [E9]. Richard Wrangham's narrower claim is not that fire invented narrative, but that cooking helped pay the energetic cost of larger brains; the storytelling leap is this essay's inference, not his finding [E10]. Benedict Anderson supplies the political half: an imagined community is not fake because it is imagined, and repeated symbols can make strangers act as a public [E11]. Campfire gave way to hearth, then stove, then screen; the fuel changed while the ring of light held [E9][E10].

That is why the cheap claim is wrong. The essay is not saying football distracts from war. War is involuntary attention, imposed by missiles, insurance, oil, and rate expectations; spectacle is voluntary attention, ritualized through kickoff, anthem, and the screen [E2][E4][E11]. The two do not merge. They rhyme in how they gather bodies around a shared signal while charging different prices for belonging.

Calendar coincidence remains the live null. July supplies heat; wars and tournaments have shared newspapers before without becoming one story [E5][E6]. Falsification is plain: if the war track and the game track do not touch beyond adjacency, the two fires are only two fires [E1][E6]. Washington struck Iranian targets while American cities hosted the tournament: oil and anthem remained on separate tracks, but inside the same national staging ground [E1][E6][E11]. Stand back far enough to see pattern and the mind starts painting dragons in the flames [E9].

American forces had completed the retaliatory round before Brent settled into the session repricing [E1][E3]. FedWatch's September odds had moved higher; FIFPRO's thresholds and FIFA's hydration policy were already on the record [E4][E5][E7]. Oil did not wait for the anthem, and the anthem did not pause for the strait [E1][E2][E11]. More than eighty strikes answered the tankers while heat risk played on schedule; neither fire asked permission from the other [E1][E5][E6].

dissent.argument: The two-fires reading is likely projection: spectacle and great-power war have run on separate tracks for a century without merging, and the World Cup heat is a scheduling-and-weather story with a mundane cause. Reading one day's front page as a single myth is the fire painting shapes on the wall.

evidence_box.fragment: retaliatory-strikes release documenting more than 80 Iranian targets after tanker attacks
interim accord declared over as oil climbed on renewed Gulf hostilities
Brent crude futures data used for the 8 July session repricing
FedWatch September move odds: 65.7%, up from 61.9% before the minutes
heat guidance: 26°C cooling-break line and 28°C postponement line
fixture-list WBGT projection: 26 of 104 matches at or above 26°C
tournament-wide hydration-break policy for player welfare
Mexico-England slot held after severe-weather schedule review
firelight talk as story, ritual, and imagined-community time
cooking and the energetic support for larger brains
imagined communities: constructed symbols can still be socially real
